title: Call Center Agent

rationales.taskAutomation: Most high-volume inbound call flows — account verification, basic troubleshooting, appointment reminders — are scripted enough for AI voice agents to run end-to-end at production quality.
rationales.toolMaturity: Conversational AI voice platforms with real-time speech and telephony integration are mature and already deployed across large call center operations.
rationales.adoption: Call centers were early, aggressive adopters of AI voice deflection given the direct link to headcount cost; adoption is near-mainstream among large operators.
rationales.agenticExposure: A call queue is close to an agent spec: answer, verify, resolve or route, log — an autonomous voice agent can traverse it end-to-end for most routine calls.

tasksAtRisk: High-volume inbound call answering and routing
Scripted account verification and basic troubleshooting
tasksGrowing: Complex or emotionally charged call handling
Supervising and correcting AI voice-agent transfers
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-4051 (Customer Service Representatives); range reflects call-center-specific roles at the lower end of the occupation band
insight: AI voice agents now run the bulk of routine call center volume end-to-end, which is why this role carries the steepest agentic exposure in the category. Agents who move into escalation handling, retention calls, and AI-voice-agent supervision are the ones still needed on the floor.
